summary: Sprint 7 delivers a complete block-based rewrite that mechanically meets the 15 grep/threshold contract criteria, but four of the five strict-mode dimensions FAIL because the implementation embeds test-harness scaffolding into production code (off-screen 'ghost' block, always-rendered hidden validation hints, hard-coded data-shadow-applied/data-accent-color attributes) and misimplements two material spec rules (Copy button disabled when isDirty contradicts REQ-EDIT-005/020/032; New-Note button disabled when editing+dirty contradicts REQ-EDIT-033/035 and EC-EDIT-008/010). The pure tier (editorReducer / editorPredicates / debounceSchedule / types) is sound; verification_readiness PASSES. The 13 findings (3 critical, 7 major, 3 minor) are routed back to Phase 2b (implementation fixes), Phase 2a (test rewrites), Phase 2c (cleanup), and Phase 1a (spec gaps in block-tree channel and reorder boundary).

evidence.description: isNewNoteDisabled fires for editing+dirty contrary to REQ-EDIT-033/035/PROP-EDIT-022
Ghost Enter handler dispatches both SplitBlock and InsertBlockAfter — XOR violation
REQ-EDIT-035 deferred RequestNewNote intent is dropped, never re-dispatched after save completes
Off-screen 'ghost block' element exists in production code purely for test queries
Always-rendered hidden block-validation-hint elements exist purely to satisfy editor-validation tests
handleSnapshot fabricates blocks from focusedBlockId when snapshot does not carry block tree
EC-EDIT-014 tautological — only asserts dispatchCancelSwitch fired, never the focus restoration
data-shadow-applied/data-accent-color attributes hard-coded on banner root, asserted instead of computed style
Pure-tier purity audit: zero forbidden-API hits
Pure-tier purity audit: zero forbidden-API hits
Pure-tier purity audit: zero forbidden-API hits
PROP-EDIT-040 has 6 grep matches (a-f) and explicit priorFocusedBlockId assertion at 040e
Editor-scope tsc clean (0 errors/warnings); CRIT-713 fallback (CRIT-703 + CRIT-706 + tsc) satisfied

convergenceSignals.criteriaResultsNote: All 15 contract thresholds pass mechanically (grep counts, file existence, test exit codes). The thresholds do not catch the spec-violating runtime logic (FIND-058, FIND-059, FIND-065) or the test-harness scaffolding embedded in production code (FIND-060, FIND-062, FIND-066). Strict-mode dimension PASS requires zero critical AND zero major; four dimensions therefore FAIL despite every CRIT threshold being met. This is a known limitation of grep/exit-code thresholds and is the precise reason adversarial review exists.
